evals.prompt: A 10km section of paved national road in Uganda has been assessed by Skill #56 with the following results: condition rating = Poor, IRI = 7.5 m/km, VCI = 38, distress summary shows alligator cracking at High severity covering 25% of the area and rutting at 22mm average depth. The road is asphalt concrete, 7m wide, 2 lanes, carrying 2,500 vpd. Select the appropriate maintenance intervention, recommend specific treatments, and estimate preliminary quantities.
evals.expected_output: Rehabilitation triggered (IRI 7.5 > 6 m/km trigger, VCI 38 < 40 trigger). Alligator cracking at High severity with 25% extent indicates structural failure. Recommended: structural investigation (FWD/DCP), then overlay design per Skill #25. Preliminary scope: shaped patching of worst failures (immediate), then AC overlay on 70,000 m2 (10km x 7m). Drainage repair must precede overlay. Refer to Skill #25 for overlay thickness and Skill #50 for cost estimate.

assertions.text: Correctly identifies rehabilitation as the intervention level based on IRI exceeding 6 m/km and VCI below 40
Identifies alligator cracking at High severity as indicating structural failure requiring more than surface treatment
Recommends structural investigation (FWD or DCP) before detailed rehabilitation design
Refers to Skill #25 (Pavement Rehabilitation) for overlay design and Skill #50 for cost estimation
Includes drainage repair as a prerequisite activity before surface treatment

evals.prompt: A 15km gravel district road in western Uganda has condition rating = Poor from visual survey (no IRI data). Distress summary: corrugation at Medium severity covering 30% of area, potholes at Low severity covering 8%, and gravel thickness loss estimated at 40%. The road is 6m wide, carrying approximately 150 vpd. The district has a limited maintenance budget of UGX 500 million for this financial year across all district roads. Select the maintenance intervention and estimate quantities for this road section.
evals.expected_output: Periodic maintenance triggered (VCI in periodic range, gravel loss 40% in periodic range 25-50%, corrugation Medium severity triggers periodic). Treatment: gravel resheeting 150mm compacted. Quantities: ~18,000 m3 loose gravel (allowing for compaction), pothole patching, drain clearing 30km. Notes budget constraint and recommends prioritization per Skill #59 if budget insufficient for full resheeting.

assertions.text: Correctly identifies periodic maintenance as the intervention level based on gravel road trigger levels
Selects gravel resheeting as the primary periodic treatment for gravel road with corrugation and gravel loss
Estimates resheeting quantities including compaction loss factor (loose volume > compacted volume)
Acknowledges budget constraint and references Skill #59 for prioritization if budget is insufficient
Includes drainage clearing as part of the maintenance plan alongside surface treatment

evals.prompt: A 50km national road corridor in Uganda has mixed conditions: 30km in Good condition (IRI < 4, VCI > 80), 15km in Fair condition (IRI 4-6, VCI 60-80), and 5km in Poor condition (IRI 6-8, VCI 40-60). Periodic maintenance has been separately programmed for the 5km Poor section. Prepare a routine maintenance plan for the entire 50km corridor, specifying activities and quantities for each maintenance type.
evals.expected_output: Routine maintenance applies to all 50km regardless of condition (including the 5km with periodic treatment programmed). Activities: pothole patching (~500 m2), crack sealing (~2,000 lm for Fair/Poor sections), drainage clearing (100 km), vegetation control (300,000 m2 at 3 cuts/year), shoulder maintenance (60 km), sign maintenance. Notes that routine maintenance continues even when periodic/rehabilitation is programmed.

assertions.text: Plans routine maintenance for the full 50km corridor, including sections with periodic maintenance programmed
Includes drainage clearing as a standard routine maintenance activity with quantity estimate
Specifies multiple routine activities (patching, crack sealing, vegetation, drainage, shoulders) with quantities
Notes that routine maintenance is always needed regardless of condition -- it is baseline, not alternative to periodic
Provides quantity estimates suitable for input to Skill #50 (Engineer's Estimate)
